name: @chbrain/khai-engine-monomyth
description: khai engine content, the monomyth domain: the hero's journey as a play -- separation, initiation, and return, run as three chained plots.
license: SEE LICENSE IN LICENSE and LICENSE-CODE

khai.tagline: The monomyth as play: a hero leaves the known world, is transformed in a region of trial, and returns carrying a boon -- separation, initiation, and return, run as three chained plots the whole production turns on.

card.wire: Attaches at Play. The anchor (play_monomyth.md) declares the hero's journey as a whole run: separation, initiation, and return, the one story Campbell found under a thousand myths. The law makes a production that installs it read its own arc against the round trip -- crossing, ordeal, and boon brought home.
card.issue: The journey, in three plots. Departure carries the call, the refusal, the mentor's aid, and the crossing of the first threshold; Initiation carries the road of trials, the ordeal in the innermost cave, and the seizing of the boon; Return carries the flight, the recrossing, and the surrender of the boon to the ordinary world. The three chain in order; each plot's close is the next one's cue.
card.require: Two altitudes hold the engine. The law: declared once in the project's Instructions Knowledge chapter, linking the anchor, so the journey is read for the whole production. The link: the production's own play links, under its Arc, the journey phase its telling rests on.
card.enforce: Structure is checked; coherence is judged. The kit verifies the floor: the law is declared and a real journey phase is linked. Whether the telling actually crosses, transforms, and returns -- rather than departing and never coming back -- is semantic, taught and reviewed, never failed by the suite.
card.setup: Declare the law in the Instructions Knowledge chapter, linking the anchor. Then wire the production's arc to the three plots: which crossing fires the departure, what ordeal the initiation turns on, and whether the boon is carried home in the return. Read the play against the round trip -- a hero who leaves and never returns transformed has not run the monomyth.
